summary: An outstanding matrix captures a legendary 1977 show, highlighted by an explosive second set featuring Estimated Prophet, Eyes of the World, The Other One and Wharf Rat.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear matrix that showcases the band at peak form; reviewers rave about its clarity and note only minor mic cutouts on Bertha. Musically the concert shines brightest in the second half, where an Estimated Prophet jam erupts into a soaring Eyes of the World → The Other One → Wharf Rat sequence that many fans call a must‑hear moment.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews are 4–5 stars and unanimously praise both the performance (especially the second set) and the matrix sound quality, with only trivial technical notes.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix that captures the band’s dynamics; minor mic cutouts on Bertha noted but not detrimental. Matrix format provides the best listening experience compared with the SBD or audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and groove‑filled, but the second set builds a monumental arc—from an Estimated Prophet jam into an escalating Eyes of the World that blossoms into The Other One and a soaring Wharf Rat—making the concert’s momentum feel like a crescendo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect tone, tasteful restraint (steps back more than typical ’77 shows) and energetic jumps on choruses such as Mexicali Blues.
